Attack Documentation

Overview

The Attack container generates expressive onset transients using physical excitation models. It produces shaped bursts that blend with an input signal during the attack portion of a note, giving each sound a distinctive physical character at note onset. Six attack types are available — Air Jet, Reed Chirp, Sub Bloom, Lip Bend, FM Strike, and Membrane Sweep — each implementing a different DSP excitation algorithm. While the underlying algorithms draw on acoustic physics, these are general-purpose transient shapers: the results depend entirely on the parameter settings and signal chain context.

Category

Essential (Blue) - Core sound generation

Ports

Input Ports

Port Name Data Type Default Description
signalIn Signal 0.0 Input signal to blend with the attack transient
trigger Trigger — Triggers a new attack excitation
attackType Control 0 Attack type (0-5, see Attack Types section)
duration Control 0.15 Duration of the attack phase in seconds (0.005-5.0)
intensity Control 0.8 Overall intensity/loudness of the attack burst (0.0-1.0)
mix Control 0.8 Prominence of the attack transient vs input signal (0.0-1.0)
noiseAmount Control 0.5 Air Jet: amount of turbulence noise (0.0-1.0)
jetRatio Control 0.32 Air Jet: jet stream instability and bandwidth (0.0-1.0)
reedStiffness Control 0.5 Reed Chirp / Sub Bloom: reed stiffness affecting buzz character (0.0-1.0)
reedAperture Control 0.5 Reed Chirp / Sub Bloom: reed opening affecting tone offset (0.0-1.0)
blowPosition Control 0.5 Sub Bloom: embouchure position affecting warmth and sub-harmonics (0.0-1.0)
lipTension Control 0.5 Lip Bend: lip tension controlling pitch bend speed (0.0-1.0)
buzzQ Control 5.0 Lip Bend: Q factor of lip resonance filter (0.5-50.0)
hardness Control 0.5 FM Strike: hardness affecting FM carrier ratio (0.0-1.0)
brightness Control 0.5 FM Strike: brightness affecting FM modulation depth (0.0-1.0)
tightness Control 0.5 Membrane Sweep: membrane tightness affecting pitch sweep and decay speed (0.0-1.0)
tone Control 0.5 Membrane Sweep: overall tone affecting pitch range and noise color (0.0-1.0)

Output Ports

Port Name Data Type Description
signalOut Signal Blended output of attack transient and input signal

Parameters

Common Parameters

Parameter Type Default Range Description
attackType Integer 0 0-5 Selects the excitation algorithm (see Attack Types)
duration Float 0.15 0.005-5.0 Attack phase duration in seconds
intensity Float 0.8 0.0-1.0 Overall intensity of the attack burst
mix Float 0.8 0.0-1.0 Prominence of attack transient vs input signal

Air Jet Parameters

Parameter Type Default Range Description
noiseAmount Float 0.5 0.0-1.0 Amount of turbulence noise
jetRatio Float 0.32 0.0-1.0 Jet stream instability and filter bandwidth

Reed Chirp / Sub Bloom Parameters

Parameter Type Default Range Description
reedStiffness Float 0.5 0.0-1.0 Reed stiffness (higher = more aggressive buzz)
reedAperture Float 0.5 0.0-1.0 Reed opening (affects tonal offset)

Sub Bloom Only

Parameter Type Default Range Description
blowPosition Float 0.5 0.0-1.0 Embouchure position (higher = warmer, more sub-harmonics)

Lip Bend Parameters

Parameter Type Default Range Description
lipTension Float 0.5 0.0-1.0 Lip tension (higher = faster pitch arrival)
buzzQ Float 5.0 0.5-50.0 Q factor of the lip resonance filter

FM Strike Parameters

Parameter Type Default Range Description
hardness Float 0.5 0.0-1.0 Hardness (higher = more inharmonic partials)
brightness Float 0.5 0.0-1.0 Brightness (higher = deeper FM modulation)

Membrane Sweep Parameters

Parameter Type Default Range Description
tightness Float 0.5 0.0-1.0 Membrane tightness (higher = faster pitch sweep and decay)
tone Float 0.5 0.0-1.0 Overall tone (higher = higher pitch range and brighter noise)

Attack Types

0: Air Jet

Generates a turbulent noise burst with pitch instability. Uses band-limited noise shaped by a jet table nonlinearity (cubic function: x³ - x) to create airy, breathy onsets. A random-walk pitch wobble decays over the duration as a faint pitched component fades in.

Key DSP elements: - Band-limited noise (2-6 kHz) for breathiness - Jet table nonlinearity (cubic function: x³ - x) modeling turbulent deflection - Random-walk pitch wobble that decays as the note “settles” - Faint pitched component that fades in

Character: Airy, breathy, with pitch wandering at onset

1: Reed Chirp

A two-phase onset: an initial high-frequency chirp at 3x the fundamental with pitch glide, followed by a reed table nonlinearity with aggressive clipping that produces a buzzy, edgy character.

Key DSP elements: - Phase 1 (first 15%): High-frequency chirp at 3x fundamental with pitch glide - Phase 2: Reed table nonlinearity with aggressive clipping - Bore resonance filter (bandpass at 3x fundamental, Q=3.0)

Character: Brief high-pitched squeal transitioning to buzzy edge

2: Sub Bloom

A slow-building onset with sub-harmonic emphasis and low-frequency pressure wobble. Uses a reed table nonlinearity with wobble modulation, bore resonance at the fundamental, and a sub-harmonic oscillator at half the fundamental frequency.

Key DSP elements: - Exponential attack envelope for gradual “bloom” - Low-frequency pressure wobble (2-5 Hz) - Reed table nonlinearity with wobble modulation - Bore resonance at fundamental (Q=6.0) for nasal character - Sub-harmonic oscillator at half the fundamental frequency - Low-pass filtering for warmth

Character: Nasal, warm, with a slow swell and sub-harmonic richness

3: Lip Bend

Starts with a noise burst, then a resonant buzz bends upward to the target pitch. The pitch begins 1.5 semitones flat and rises at a rate controlled by lipTension. A bandpass lip resonance filter tracks the bending pitch.

Key DSP elements: - Initial noise burst (first 5% of duration) - Upward pitch bend from 1.5 semitones flat, speed controlled by lipTension - BiQuad bandpass lip resonance filter tracking the bent pitch - Asymmetric clipping (x * |x|) for harmonic generation - Faint pitched oscillation that strengthens as the resonance locks on

Character: Buzzy, with upward pitch scoop and initial burst

4: FM Strike

An FM synthesis burst with a very fast onset (1ms). The carrier-to-modulator frequency ratio is controlled by hardness (1x-15x fundamental), and the modulation index decays with the envelope — producing brighter content at the moment of impact that dulls as it fades.

Key DSP elements: - FM synthesis with carrier ratio controlled by hardness (1x-15x fundamental) - Dynamic modulation index that decays with envelope (brighter at impact) - Very fast attack (1ms) with longer decay - Subtle noise component for percussive impact

Character: Percussive, metallic, with variable brightness

5: Membrane Sweep

A membrane-like impact with downward pitch sweep and body resonance. The oscillator starts at a high frequency and sweeps exponentially down to a rest frequency. Includes an independent noise burst and a low-frequency body resonance.

Key DSP elements: - Membrane oscillator with exponential downward pitch sweep - Noise burst filtered by tone parameter - Body resonance (BiQuad bandpass at 80-280 Hz, Q=3.0) - Independent decay rates for membrane, noise, and body components

Character: Thump with pitch drop, variable from tight snap to boomy

Signal Blending

The Attack container blends its excitation burst with the input signal using a crossfade that shifts from burst to input over the duration of the attack:

burstWeight = (1.0 - progress) * mix
inputWeight = 1.0 - burstWeight
output = burst * burstWeight + inputSignal * inputWeight

A cosine fade is applied over the final 10% of the attack phase to prevent clicks at the transition point.

This means: - At the start of the note: mostly attack transient - At the end of the attack phase: mostly input signal - After the attack phase: pure input signal passes through

Parameter Effects

Duration

Controls the length of the attack phase:

Value Effect
0.005-0.02 Extremely short, percussive click
0.05-0.1 Short, snappy onset
0.15 (default) Natural-feeling onset
0.3-0.5 Extended, dramatic onset
1.0-5.0 Very slow build, atmospheric

Intensity

Controls the overall level of the attack burst:

Value Effect
0.0-0.2 Subtle, barely audible transient
0.5 Moderate attack presence
0.8 (default) Strong, clear attack
1.0 Maximum intensity

Mix

Controls how much the attack transient dominates over the input signal:

Value Effect
0.0 No attack effect — input signal passes through unchanged
0.3-0.5 Subtle attack coloring
0.8 (default) Prominent attack character
1.0 Maximum attack prominence

Usage Examples

Adding Air Jet to a Harmonic Sound

[Harmonic Generator] → [Rolloff] → [Spectrum to Signal] → signalIn
                                                               ↓
                                                          [Attack] → signalOut
                                                           attackType: 0 (Air Jet)
                                                           duration: 0.15
                                                           noiseAmount: 0.6
                                                           jetRatio: 0.4

The harmonic generator provides the sustained tone body, while the Attack container adds a breathy jet onset.

Lip Bend Onset with Envelope Shaping


[Harmonic Generator] → [Spectrum to Signal] → signalIn
                                                   ↓
[Envelope Engine] → duration ──────────────→ [Attack] → signalOut
                                               attackType: 3 (Lip Bend)
                                               lipTension: 0.7
                                               buzzQ: 8.0

An Envelope Engine modulates the attack duration for expressive pitch-bending entrances.

FM Strike Attack

[Harmonic Generator] → [Spectrum to Signal] → signalIn
                                                   ↓
                                              [Attack] → signalOut
                                               attackType: 4 (FM Strike)
                                               duration: 0.08
                                               hardness: 0.7
                                               brightness: 0.6

Short duration with high hardness creates a punchy, bright percussive impact.

Membrane Sweep with Noise Color


[Noise Color Filter] → signalIn
                            ↓
                       [Attack] → signalOut
                        attackType: 5 (Membrane Sweep)
                        tightness: 0.8
                        tone: 0.3

The membrane sweep adds a pitch-dropping transient to a noise texture.

Configuration (Preferences)

Setting Default Description
durationMin 0.005 Minimum attack duration in seconds
durationMax 5.0 Maximum attack duration in seconds
buzzQMin 0.5 Minimum brass buzz Q factor
buzzQMax 50.0 Maximum brass buzz Q factor

Technical Notes

Output Normalization

All attack types normalize their output to a target peak of 0.8 to maintain consistent levels across attack types and parameter settings.

RNG Seeding

Each trigger seeds the random number generator with a pitch-dependent value, ensuring that the same note at the same pitch produces consistent attack character, while different pitches get naturally varied excitations.

Pitch Tracking

Pitch is clamped to the range 20-20000 Hz internally. Attack types that involve pitch-dependent elements (bore resonance, lip resonance, membrane sweep) automatically track the note’s fundamental frequency.

Parameter Applicability

Not all parameters affect every attack type. Each parameter group is specific to its instrument family:

Parameter Air Jet Reed Chirp Sub Bloom Lip Bend FM Strike Membrane Sweep
noiseAmount X




jetRatio X




reedStiffness
X X


reedAperture
X X


blowPosition

X


lipTension


X

buzzQ


X

hardness



X
brightness



X
tightness




X
tone




X

Troubleshooting

No Audible Attack

  1. Verify the trigger signal is being received (connect a trigger source or ensure note events are flowing)
  2. Check that intensity is not set to 0
  3. Check that mix is not set to 0
  4. Increase duration — very short durations may be imperceptible

Attack Too Harsh

  1. Reduce intensity
  2. Reduce mix to blend more input signal
  3. For Air Jet: reduce noiseAmount
  4. For Lip Bend: reduce buzzQ
  5. For FM Strike: reduce hardness and brightness

Attack Too Subtle

  1. Increase intensity toward 1.0
  2. Increase mix toward 1.0
  3. Increase duration for a longer, more noticeable onset

Input Signal Cuts Out During Attack

This is expected behavior — the attack transient crossfades with the input signal. The input signal fully returns after the attack phase completes. To maintain more of the input signal during the attack, reduce mix.

Clicks at Attack End

  1. This should not occur due to the built-in cosine fade. If it does, try slightly longer duration values.

This documentation reflects the current implementation in attackgenerator.h and attackgenerator.cpp.
